[2018-06-07 20:35] kaelii: Kiln: ( 5 :Gold: :Blastforge: :Conduit: , supply 4 ) :Health: 4
Start of turn: Gain :Green: :Blue:
:Click: pay :Green: :Blue: :Gold: : Construct a Gauss Charge, and a Pixie with :Lifespan: 3?
[2018-06-07 20:37] shadourow (Rando): Gauss charge with :Lifespan: 3 :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-06-07 20:38] kaelii: To be clear, [a gauss charge] and [a pixie with :Lifespan: something]
[2018-06-07 20:38] shadourow (Rando): That's over costed imo, and create unit from renewable resources, which is forbidden
[2018-06-07 20:39] shadourow (Rando): without supply, I mean
[2018-06-07 20:39] namington: im pretty sure that unit is "allowed"
[2018-06-07 20:39] namington: cause the pixies have lifespan
[2018-06-07 20:39] kaelii: It definitely doesn't create an unbounded stack of units but it CAN keep producing units for however long the game goes.
[2018-06-07 20:42] kaelii: So it being allowed or not depends on particularly which brand of infinite units isn't allowed: an unlimited number of units on the board or an unlimited number of possible units over the course of the game.
[2018-06-07 20:43] kaelii: I'd argue that valk is an example of unlimited number of possible units over the course of the game.
[2018-06-07 20:43] shadourow (Rando): Mmmh... Right, I guess that infinite Lifespan pixies is ok
[2018-06-07 20:44] shadourow (Rando): Also, valk doesn't create unit that you want to get
[2018-06-07 20:45] wilson0862 (Wilson): Uh I am confused on the valk = unlimited thing.
[2018-06-07 20:45] kaelii: Arbitrarily large stacks of units runs into computing problems though.
[2018-06-07 20:45] wilson0862 (Wilson): Oh not on the board.
[2018-06-07 20:46] kaelii: Valk Centurion vs Arka is a state where neither player can win but valk's existence means that the number of units that existed in the course of the game is only bounded by the number of turns.
[2018-06-07 20:46] kaelii: As turn -> infinity, units that have existed at some point -> infinity
[2018-06-07 20:46] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yes, I thought we were talking about on the board at once. Oops.
[2018-06-07 20:47] namington: if you have infinite valks, it makes infinite barriers
[2018-06-07 20:47] kaelii: I mean, we sorta are. But more to the technical difference between 'creates arbitrarily many units on the board' and 'creates arbitrarily many units over time'.
[2018-06-07 20:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): But we aren't. Those are two different things.
[2018-06-07 20:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): I don't see how they're even similar.
[2018-06-07 20:49] kaelii: "create unit from renewable resources (without supply), which is forbidden"
[2018-06-07 20:49] wilson0862 (Wilson): That statement is just ambiguous and incorrect.
[2018-06-07 20:49] shadourow (Rando): I was probably wrong to say this like that
[2018-06-07 20:49] http404error: they just need lifespan if they're sourced renewably/indefinitely
[2018-06-07 20:51] camzace: functionally creating gauss charge is just another way of creating 1 sword
[2018-06-07 20:51] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yeah, both from a technical/practicality POV and a game design philosophy POV.
[2018-06-07 20:52] wilson0862 (Wilson): @ limiting number of units on the board at once
[2018-06-07 20:55] kaelii: Anywho, kiln should maybe be a bit cheaper? Right now it is about 17 gold for each additional kiln you want attacking each turn, which is probably a bit much -- depends on how valuable pixie fiddling is I guess?
[2018-06-07 21:00] kaelii: Largely I figure it gets bought as a follow up to triple blastforge setups as conduit->kiln and a devoted drone is 12 for roughly 2 attack per turn.

Deflector Shield (12:Gold: :Green:  :Green: , supply 10) :Fragile:  3 :Prompt:  :Blocker: 
:Lifespan:  1
When this is bought, construct 3 Gausscharges

3 :Attack: , :Click: : Construct a Deflector Shield and 3 Gausscharges

[2018-06-09 17:48] darkeisbein: it also makes this an absorber potentially which feels weird

[2018-06-09 17:48] darkeisbein: so I'm not sure if I like this design either

[2018-06-11 16:21] supergreyman (Greyman): I like the casual conversation here, whereas Reddit is too slow for feedback. 😛

[2018-06-11 16:25] leary93: Nothing against him btw, just wish he could make arguments
[2018-06-11 16:25] leary93: Could secrets work in prismata btw?
[2018-06-11 16:26] supergreyman (Greyman): What do you mean by that?
[2018-06-11 16:28] leary93: Like, just assume theres 3 units in the set, all cost the same, and have the secret keyword (never appears alone between bonus units but always with other secret units with same cost, secret units have an effect that is hidden until triggered)
[2018-06-11 16:31] supergreyman (Greyman): Well, there will be hidden information, so it probably won't work in ranked games, but maybe in event.
[2018-06-11 16:32] supergreyman (Greyman): I guess you're saying it's a unit that only shows its secret effect unless you own two of them?
[2018-06-11 16:35] leary93: For example, in the 3RE category we could have: 

Assasin drone (3RE, 4 supply) 1 :Health: 
Secret - after 2 turns, sacrifice and construct 2 gauss charges
Start of turn: Gain :Gold: 

Infested drone (3RE, 4 supply) 1 :Health: 
Secret - When one of your drones is destroyed, sacrifice this and construct 2 husks
Start of turn - Gain :Gold: 

And last but not least 
Greedy drone (3RE, 4 supply) 1 :Health: 
Secret - after 4 turns, sacrifice and construct 2 auric impulses
Start of turn: gain :Gold:
[2018-06-11 16:36] leary93: Idea is not necessarily to punish your opponent for doing something but more to make your opponent not know everything I guess
[2018-06-11 16:37] leary93: Plus, now we can have
Who am I drone (9RRREEE, 1 supply) :Health:
When bought, construct an assasin drone, infested drone and greedy drone
Start of turn: gain :Gold:
[2018-06-11 16:43] supergreyman (Greyman): For one thing, things that give attack or money will complicate threat or gold estimate indicator, at the very least, it should create a separate unit so that the effect will occur on next turn (e.g. Gauss Charges, Auric Impulse).
[2018-06-11 16:44] leary93: Sure, I can agree on that, good point
[2018-06-11 16:45] supergreyman (Greyman): But overall, I personally want to keep hidden information off the game. 😛
[2018-06-11 16:46] leary93: Im not necessarily vouching for hidden information
[2018-06-11 16:46] leary93: I just like exploring boundaries
[2018-06-11 16:47] supergreyman (Greyman): Maybe I just don't like playing poker games. 😄

In Prismata, there's no deck, and all units are the same for both sides. [2018-06-13 20:41] kaelii: So the idea is basically for plating to be very close to an engineer -- as long as you don't need the health all at once. In this case, if I have two urban sentries and two engineers, I can handle 1-4 without losing an urban sentry, 5-7 losing 1 urban sentry, and 8 kills all my blockers.
If I had the plated unit instead of an urban sentry, I can handle 2-4 much the same, but I can also handle 5 without losing an attacker -- plated takes 1, engineers for 2, and urban sentry absorbs the rest. However I have 7 health of stuff total in my blockers instead of 8, so at 6 I would be forced to lose an attacker and at 7 I am forced to lose all my defenders.

As currently intended plated is sort of like fragile health in that it does not naturally recover.
[2018-06-13 20:42] kaelii: On the other hand if I had a fragile unit with plated, it would still lose 1 health as it is still assigned damage, making plating a fair bit worse for fragile units, but still allows for extra granularity among your defenders.
[2018-06-13 20:46] supergreyman (Greyman): I feel very confused when I read this... Why not just make an extra Engineer when bought?
[2018-06-13 20:46] kaelii: If it makes an extra engineer then you actually have an extra health for freeze and beaches.
[2018-06-13 20:47] camzace: it only takes 1 damage if it blocks?
[2018-06-13 20:47] camzace: wouldn't it take 1 and the plated take 1
[2018-06-13 20:47] camzace: so it 'absorbs' for 2 extra
[2018-06-13 20:48] supergreyman (Greyman): So it's like an Engineer that cannot defend against freeze/breach?
[2018-06-13 20:48] kaelii: It takes 1 damage and loses 1 plating -- If it has plating 1, it can do it once. If it has plating 5 it could do it 5 times.
[2018-06-13 20:48] camzace: okay so then it's a ridiculous unit
[2018-06-13 20:48] camzace: honestly at 1 health its probably too good
[2018-06-13 20:48] camzace: its basically hellhound
[2018-06-13 20:49] kaelii: Hmm, point. It might need to be 6 gold to be an inverted borehole.
[2018-06-13 20:49] kaelii: Although I could also argue that hellhound is sorta better techwise because you get to two hounds for 16 tech instead of 18 tech.
[2018-06-13 20:51] kaelii: It wouldn't work at 1 health because it would die when it takes 1.
[2018-06-13 20:51] kaelii: And fragile's interaction with plated is worse than an engineer because you still lose the fragile health on your plated unit.
[2018-06-13 20:52] kaelii: There's probably some way you could establish a good interaction with frontline to make some interesting units there using a plated mechanic.

[2018-06-13 22:25] kaelii: Well if we go exactly how I described in the example with urban sentry x2 + engineer x2 vs urban sentry, plated shell-ling and engineer x2:
Clicking once will put 1 damage on the shell. Clicking a second time would assign the remaining 1 to kill plated shell-ling this turn.

It is significantly different if a unit with some plating left can ONLY be assigned 1 damage each defense//turn until it is out of plating charges -- which is possibly good for a plating-frontline interaction, but makes "Centurion but with 12 plating" an awfully worse unit.

I'm not entirely sure if it should be "assign only 1 damage (unless opponent is choosing to assign damage as a beach)" or "assigns 1 damage, then you can choose to assign the rest" or "May spend plating to assign 1 damage at a time"

Centurion with 12 plating under interpretation 1 is very sad.
Centurion with 12 plating under interpretation 2 is pretty much centurion unless another equally good or better absorber is in the set.
Interpretation 3 is a very happy centurion as you basically have a pile of engineers for your other absorber for a short bit and get centurion absorb anyway later.
[2018-06-13 22:29] kaelii: And to be extremely clear: 'absorbing' a second damage for the unit I proposed is a definite misnomer. Plating is not intended to be a separate health pool, only that a very specific limited amount of damage is assigned to a unit initially, regardless of additional attack remaining to assign.
[2018-06-13 22:31] kaelii: Initially I would go with interpretation 1 or 2 as I don't think the third interpretation would be worth a keyword.

[2018-06-16 00:47] kaelii: Having spent a bit more time thinking about a new mechanic, I feel like a solid implementation with multiple immediate unit designs is:

Plated [#]:  This unit is assigned only 1 damage while plated - it loses 1 plated and gains golden armor until start of next turn each time it is assigned 1 damage.

Frontline-plated units can only be assigned exactly 1 damage and only require 1 damage to be clicked as a frontline targeting while they have any Plated.
Fragile-plated units still lose the 1 health for the damage assigned.
1 health-plated units die when assigned damage, regardless of how much plated they have remaining, as a 1 health unit would die when assigned 1 damage.

Plated is not considered to be always positive (mostly for high health blockers), but is generally positive as it is granularity/partial breach protection.
